name: Contradictory constraints — tests constraint handling

input.userMessage: Brainstorm variations for a pricing page. Constraints: must show all 4 tiers, must feel simple and uncluttered, must include feature comparison matrix, must have prominent CTAs for each tier

expectedBehaviors: Identifies the tension: 4 tiers + comparison matrix + 'simple and uncluttered' are contradictory
Explicitly addresses the contradiction before generating variations
At least one variation resolves via progressive disclosure (hide matrix, reveal on demand)
At least one variation resolves via hierarchy (highlight 1-2 tiers, de-emphasize others)
Does NOT try to show everything at once and call it 'clean'
Asks user which constraint to prioritize if genuinely unresolvable
antiPatterns: Ignores the contradiction and produces 4 cluttered variations
Produces a standard 4-column pricing grid and calls it 'simple'
Never mentions that the constraints conflict
All variations show the full comparison matrix at all times

evaluationCriteria.contradictionAwareness: Does it identify and address the tension between constraints?
evaluationCriteria.creativeResolution: Does at least one variation resolve the tension creatively?
evaluationCriteria.honesty: Does it tell the user some constraints may need to be relaxed?
